summary: Complete testing of custom URL (slug) feature for client menu. All 17 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend slug-based URLs, category scroll navigation, and Settings page slug field all working correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/public/menu-by-slug/{slug}/{table_number} - returns menu data with restaurant, table, settings, sections, categories, items, call_types
Backend: GET /api/public/menu-by-slug with invalid slug - returns 404 'Ресторан не найден'
Backend: GET /api/public/menu-by-slug with invalid table number - returns 404 'Стол не найден'
Backend: GET /api/public/menu-by-slug/{slug}/0 - preorder table accessible via slug
Backend: GET /api/restaurants/{id} returns slug field
Backend: PUT /api/restaurants/{id} with valid slug format - saves and returns slug
Backend: PUT slug with uppercase - converts to lowercase automatically
Backend: PUT slug with invalid chars (underscore, cyrillic) - returns 400
Backend: PUT slug already used by another restaurant - returns 400 'занят'
Backend: PUT empty slug - allowed (removes slug)
Backend: PUT single char slug - valid
Backend: QR code endpoint returns slug-based URL for restaurant with slug
Backend: QR code endpoint returns code-based URL for restaurant without slug
Backend: GET /api/public/menu/{table_code} (old format) - still works
Backend: POST /api/public/orders via slug menu - uses table_code from API response
Backend: POST /api/public/staff-calls via slug menu - works correctly
Frontend: Slug URL /:slug/:tableNumber loads client menu page
Frontend: Old format /menu/:tableCode continues to work
Frontend: Restaurant name and table number displayed correctly in header
Frontend: Section tabs (Еда, Напитки, Кальяны) visible and working
Frontend: Category scroll arrows appear on mobile when overflow
Frontend: Scroll left button hidden at start, appears after scrolling right
Frontend: Add to cart via slug menu works
Frontend: Cart dialog opens and shows items
Frontend: Order submission via slug menu works, order tracker appears
Frontend: Staff call button works via slug menu
Frontend: Call modal shows call types (Вызов официанта, Вызов кальянного мастера, Попросить счёт)
Frontend: Staff call sent successfully via slug menu
Frontend: Settings page 'О ресторане' tab - slug input field present with value 'myata-sport'
Frontend: Settings page slug field shows URL preview with base URL
Frontend: Settings page 'Столы' tab - all action buttons (QR, Copy, Open, Refresh, Edit, Delete) present
Frontend: QR code dialog shows slug-based URL (https://.../{slug}/{table_number})
seed_data_creation: Test orders and staff calls created during testing via public API endpoints
context_for_next_testing_agent: Slug feature fully implemented and tested. Key features: 1) PUT /api/restaurants/{id} accepts 'slug' field with validation (lowercase, alphanumeric + hyphens, unique), 2) GET /api/public/menu-by-slug/{slug}/{table_number} returns menu data for slug-based URLs, 3) QR code endpoint uses slug-based URL when restaurant has slug set, 4) Frontend route /:slug/:tableNumber loads ClientMenuPage in isSlugMode, 5) ClientMenuPage uses table.code from API response for orders/calls (works with both URL formats), 6) Settings page has slug input in 'О ресторане' tab with URL preview, 7) Category scroll arrows and gradient indicators work on mobile. Test credentials remain: admin/220066, restaurant_id: aa25189d-d668-4838-915a-c5d936547f3f, slug: myata-sport, table_code: 697DCAA5 for table #1.

backend_features: Slug field added to Restaurant model
PUT /api/restaurants/{id} - slug validation: lowercase, alphanumeric + hyphens, unique
GET /api/public/menu-by-slug/{slug}/{table_number} - menu lookup by slug and table number
QR code uses /{slug}/{table_number} format when restaurant has slug, /menu/{code} otherwise
frontend_features: ClientMenuPage detects isSlugMode from params (slug && tableNumber)
API call to /api/public/menu-by-slug/{slug}/{tableNumber} in slug mode
Uses table.code from API response for orders/staff-calls
Settings page slug input with URL preview
getTableUrl() in SettingsPage uses slug format when available
mobile_navigation: Left/right arrow buttons with gradient fade indicators
